= HK-47 =

{{CharacterInfobox
| name        = HK-47
| portrait    = https://foundry.atkennedy.com/modules/pf2e-ap178-punks-in-a-powderkeg/assets/art/PZO90178 Clockwork Guard.webp?1779470078139
| level       = 8
| size        = Medium
| hp          = 101/101
| ac          = 25
| perception  = +13
| speed       = 25 ft.
| fortitude   = +15
| reflex      = +16
| will        = +11
| attributes  = '''STR''' +4 | '''DEX''' +4 | '''CON''' +3 | '''INT''' +0 | '''WIS''' +1 | '''CHA''' +2
| key_ability  = STR
| hero_points  = 0
| wealth      = 135 GP, 60 SP, 1069 CP
| last_downed = Pyronite Ooze (2026-05-18)
}}

== Details ==
* '''Ancestry:''' Automaton
* '''Heritage:''' Sharpshooter Automaton
* '''Background:''' Banished Brighite
* '''Class:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/classes/fighter.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fighter" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Fighter
* '''Key Ability:''' STR
* '''XP:''' 735

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>⚔ Defense & Saves</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
* '''HP:''' 101/101
* '''AC:''' 25
* '''Perception:''' +13 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Speed:''' 25 ft. (land)
* '''Fortitude:''' +15 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Reflex:''' +16 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Will:''' +11 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>📊 Ability Scores</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; width:100%;"
|-
! STR !! DEX !! CON !! INT !! WIS !! CHA
|-
| +4 || +4 || +3 || +0 || +1 || +2
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎓 Skills</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="width:100%;"
! Skill !! Modifier !! Proficiency
|-
| Acrobatics || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Arcana || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Athletics || +16 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Brigh Lore || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Crafting || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Deception || +14 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Diplomacy || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Intimidation || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Medicine || +1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Nature || +1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Occultism || +0 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Performance || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Religion || +11 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Society || +10 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Stealth || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Survival || +11 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Thievery || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>💰 Wealth & Carry</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable"
! Coin !! Total Value !! Bulk !! Invested
|-
| 135 GP, 60 SP, 1069 CP || 151.69 gp || 1275.5 / 9 enc / 14 max || 0/10
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>✨ Feats & Features</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Heritage=====
; '''Sharpshooter Automaton'''
: Your lithe, humanoid shape is designed for speed and accuracy suitable for ranged combat. You gain the Automaton Aim action.

=====Background Features=====
; '''Banished Brighite'''
: Once, you were a dedicated and important priest of Brigh, goddess of clockwork and invention, based out of the largest church to your deity, the Temple of Brigh in Alkenstar. Here, you made important discoveries in the annals of your religion's canon and furthered the Bronze Lady's agendas of innovation and creation. Your future seemed bright, and you predicted that it was only a matter of time until you ascended to the rank of primary cog or, someday, grand cog.

Then, your years of devout service were undone in an instant. A man named Ambrost Mugland came to the Temple and demanded not only access to the religious formulas you'd been uncovering, but exclusive rights to the precious knowledge. When you refused, Mugland sent word to the church's leaders that you'd been meddling in heretical doctrine. He also implicated you in the theft of church funds that had mysteriously gone missing as of late. Sure enough, you found yourself being asked to hand in your robes. Your church colleagues didn't turn you in, but they told you in no uncertain terms that you wouldn't be able to find sanctuary for your alleged crimes in the halls of the Temple of Brigh.

On the run and with nowhere to turn, your faith has been thoroughly tested, though so far you've remained true to your religion and maintained your sacred skills and divine powers. It's unclear what the Bronze Lady has in store for you, but at least one thing's for certain: Ambrost Mugland will pay for what he did.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to '''Intelligence''' or '''Wisdom''', and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the Religion skill and the Brigh Lore skill. You gain the Quick Identification skill feat.

=====Feats=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Advantageous Assault" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Advantageous Assault''' <small>(fighter, press)</small>
: After compromising your enemy's movement, you deliver a more deadly blow. Make a Strike against a creature that is Grabbed, Prone, or Restrained. You gain a circumstance bonus to damage on this Strike equal to the number of weapon damage dice, or that number + 2 if you're wielding the weapon in two hands. The Strike gains the following failure effect.

'''Failure''' You deal damage to the target equal to the number of weapon damage dice, or that number + 2 if you're wielding the weapon in two hands. This damage has the same damage type as the weapon.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Alkenstar Agent Dedication" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Alkenstar Agent Dedication''' <small>(archetype, dedication)</small>
: '''Access''' You have been initiated by the grand duchess of Alkenstar or one of her agents.

You put on the persona of a rough and tumble outlaw, but your real goals are to report threats to Alkenstar back to the grand duchess. You become an expert in Deception and trained in your choice of Underworld Lore or Legal Lore; if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. You gain the Lie to Me skill feat.

'''Special''' You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the Alkenstar Agent archetype.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Arcane Safeguards" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Arcane Safeguards''' <small>(automaton)</small>
: The magic powering you makes it difficult for outside magic to affect you. You gain the Resist Magic reaction.

'''Enhancement''' Your safeguards improve. When you Resist Magic, you gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to the spell rank of the triggering effect.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Assisting Shot" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Assisting Shot''' <small>(fighter, press)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You are wielding a ranged weapon.

Using your last shot to gauge your accuracy, you can fire off a quick shot to interfere with a foe in combat. Make a Strike with a ranged weapon. If the Strike hits, the next creature other than you to attack the same target before the start of your next turn gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their roll, or a +2 circumstance bonus if your Strike was a critical hit.

Effect: Assisting Shot
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Assurance (Athletics)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Assurance (Athletics)''' <small>(fortune, general, skill)</small>
: Even in the worst circumstances, you can perform basic tasks. Choose a skill you're trained in. You can forgo rolling a skill check for that skill to instead receive a result of 10 + your proficiency bonus (do not apply any other bonuses, penalties, or modifiers).

'''Special''' You can select this feat multiple times. Each time, choose a different skill and gain the benefits for that skill.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/tech/cog-brass.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Automaton Core" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Automaton Core''' <small>(automaton)</small>
: Your body contains an automaton core infused with planar quintessence that grants you power to perform various tasks and houses your soul and life energy. This life energy flows through you much like the blood of humanoids. As a result, you are a living creature. You don't have the typical construct immunities, can be affected by effects that target a living creature, and can recover Hit Points normally via vitality energy. Additionally, you are not destroyed when reduced to 0 Hit Points. Instead, your life energy attempts to keep you active even in dire straits; you are knocked out and begin dying when reduced to 0 Hit Points.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/environment/people/charge.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Battlefield Surveyor" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Battlefield Surveyor''' <small>(fighter)</small>
: Whether taking stock of an enemy army or simply standing guard, you excel at observing your foes. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master. In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks for initiative, making you faster to react during combat.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/flags/banner-standard-tattered-red.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Bravery" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Bravery''' <small>(fighter)</small>
: Having faced countless foes and the chaos of battle, you have learned how to stand strong in the face of fear. Your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to expert. When you roll a success at a Will save against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. In addition, anytime you gain the Frightened condition, reduce its value by 1.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/expert-overdrive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Constructed Body" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Constructed Body''' <small>(automaton)</small>
: Your physiological needs are different than those of living creatures. You don't need to eat or drink. You don't need to sleep, but you still need a daily period of rest. During this period of rest, you must enter a recuperating standby state for 2 hours, which is similar to sleeping except you are aware of your surroundings and don't take penalties for being Unconscious. Much like with sleeping, if you go too long without entering your standby state, you become Fatigued and can't recover until you enter standby for 2 hours.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/melee/hand-grip-sword-orange.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fighter Weapon Mastery (Firearm)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Fighter Weapon Mastery (Firearm)''' <small>(fighter)</small>
: Hours spent training with your preferred weapons, learning and developing new combat techniques, have made you particularly effective with your weapons of choice. Choose one weapon group. Your proficiency rank increases to master with the simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks in that group, and to expert with the advanced weapons in that group. You gain access to the critical specialization effects of all weapons and unarmed attacks for which you have master proficiency.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Lie to Me" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Lie to Me''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You can use Deception to weave traps to trip up anyone trying to deceive you. If you can engage in conversation with someone trying to Lie to you, use your Deception DC if it is higher than your Perception DC to determine whether they succeed. This doesn't apply if you don't have a back-and-forth dialogue, such as when someone attempts to Lie during a long speech.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Mobile Shot Stance" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Mobile Shot Stance''' <small>(fighter, stance)</small>
: Your shots become nimble and deadly. While you're in this stance, your ranged Strikes don't trigger Reactive Strike or other reactions that are triggered by a ranged attack.

'''Special''' If you have Reactive Strike, you can use it with a loaded ranged weapon you're wielding. The triggering creature must be within 5 feet of you for you to do so.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Numb to Death" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Numb to Death''' <small>(general)</small>
: '''Frequency''' once per day

Your past has left you numb to death's call. The first time each day that you regain Hit Points while you are dying, you gain a circumstance bonus to the number of Hit Points you regain equal to your level, and you neither gain the wounded condition nor increase the value of this condition.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Quick Identification" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Quick Identification''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You can Identify Magic swiftly. You take only 1 minute when using Identify Magic to determine the properties of an item, ongoing effect, or location, rather than 10 minutes. If you're a master, it takes a 3-action activity, and if you're legendary, it takes 1 action.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/melee/strike-hammer-destructive-orange.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Reactive Strike" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Reactive Strike''' <small>(fighter)</small>
: Ever watchful for weaknesses, you can quickly attack foes that leave an opening in their defenses. You gain the Reactive Strike reaction.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Recognize Threat" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Recognize Threat''' <small>(archetype, skill)</small>
: '''Requirements''' You have master proficiency in a skill to identify the triggering creature.

'''Trigger''' Your first turn of an encounter begins, and you can see a creature.

You quickly assess the threat so you can relay the information to your team. Attempt a Recall Knowledge check against the triggering creature.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Reinforced Chassis" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Reinforced Chassis''' <small>(automaton)</small>
: Your body is designed to be particularly resilient. Your chassis is medium armor in the plate armor group that grants a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of -2, a speed penalty of -5 feet, and a Strength value of +3. You can never wear other armor or remove your chassis; however, you still don't become Fatigued from sleeping. Finally, you can etch armor runes onto your chassis as normal.

'''Enhancement''' Your chassis becomes difficult to overcome. You gain the Chassis Deflection reaction.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Shield Block" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Shield Block''' <small>(general)</small>
: '''Trigger''' While you have your shield raised, you would take physical damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) from an attack.

You snap your shield in place to ward off a blow. Your shield prevents you from taking an amount of damage up to the shield's Hardness. You and the shield each take any remaining damage, possibly breaking or destroying the shield.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/shield/round-wooden-boss-gold-brown.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Shield Block" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Shield Block''' <small>(champion, druid, exemplar, fighter, inventor)</small>
: You gain the Shield Block general feat, a reaction that lets you reduce damage with your shield.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Slam Down" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Slam Down''' <small>(fighter, flourish)</small>
: You make an attack to knock a foe off balance, then follow up immediately with a sweep to topple them. Make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an Athletics check to Trip the creature you hit. If you're wielding a two-handed melee weapon, you can ignore Trip's requirement that you have a hand free. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both of them.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Slippery Prey" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Slippery Prey''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You're able to escape bonds more easily than others. When you attempt to Escape using Acrobatics or Athletics, you reduce the multiple attack penalty for repeated attempts to –4 and –8 if you're trained in the skill. The penalty becomes –3 and –6 if you're a master in the appropriate skill. If you're legendary in the skill, you don't take penalties for multiple Escape attempts in the same turn. Regardless of your training, your attempts to Escape still have the attack trait and incur a multiple attack penalty with other actions.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Sudden Charge" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Sudden Charge''' <small>(barbarian, fighter, flourish)</small>
: With a quick sprint, you dash up to your foe and swing. Stride twice. If you end your movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, you can make a melee Strike against that enemy. You can use Sudden Charge while Burrowing, Climbing, Flying, or Swimming instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Toughness" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Toughness''' <small>(general)</small>
: Your body can withstand more punishment than most before succumbing. Increase your maximum Hit Points by your level. You reduce the DC of recovery checks by 1.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Wary Disarmament" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Wary Disarmament''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: If you trigger a device or set off a trap while disarming it, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC or saving throw against the device or trap. This applies only to attacks or effects triggered by your failed attempt, not to any later ones, such as additional attacks from a complex trap.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/weapon-specialization.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Weapon Specialization" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Weapon Specialization''' <small>(alchemist, animist, bard, champion, cleric, commander, druid, fighter, gunslinger, inventor, investigator, kineticist, ranger, rogue, sorcerer, summoner, swashbuckler, witch, wizard)</small>
: You've learned how to inflict greater injuries with the weapons you know best. You deal an additional 2 damage with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you are an expert. This damage increases to 3 if you're a master, and 4 if you're legendary.

=====Actions=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Automaton Aim" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Automaton Aim'''
: You steady your body and observe the events of the battlefield to maximize the range of your next shot. You reduce the penalty for firing into your weapon's second range increment from -2 to 0 for the next ranged attack you make this turn. You can use this action a second time in the same turn to reduce the penalty from firing into your weapon's third range increment from -4 to 0 for the next ranged attack you make this turn.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Avoid Notice" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Avoid Notice''' <small>(exploration)</small>
: You attempt a Stealth check to avoid notice while traveling at half speed. If you're Avoiding Notice at the start of an encounter, you usually roll a Stealth check instead of a Perception check both to determine your initiative and to see if the enemies notice you (based on their Perception DCs, as normal for Sneak, regardless of their initiative check results).
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Reaction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Chassis Deflection" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Chassis Deflection''' <small>(automaton)</small>
: '''Trigger''' A critical hit deals physical damage to you.

Attempt a @Check[flat|dc:17]. If you are successful, the attack becomes a normal hit.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Investigate" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Investigate''' <small>(concentrate, exploration)</small>
: You seek out information about your surroundings while traveling at half speed. You use Recall Knowledge as a secret check to discover clues among the various things you can see and engage with as you journey along. You can use any skill that has a Recall Knowledge action while Investigating, but the GM determines whether the skill is relevant to the clues you could find.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Reaction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Reactive Strike" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Reactive Strike'''
: '''Trigger''' A creature within your reach uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using.

You lash out at a foe that leaves an opening. Make a melee Strike against the triggering creature. If your attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, you disrupt that action. This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Reaction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Resist Magic" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Resist Magic'''
: '''Trigger''' You attempt a saving throw against a harmful magical effect but haven't rolled yet.

Your innate magic protects you. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the triggering saving throw. Additionally, if the triggering effect is arcane, if you roll a success, you get a critical success instead.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Passive.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Scout" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Scout''' <small>(concentrate, exploration)</small>
: You scout ahead and behind the group to watch danger, moving at half speed. At the start of the next encounter, every creature in your party gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls.

Effect: Scout

</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎒 Inventory</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Weapons=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/alchemical-items/alchemical-bombs/alchemists-fire.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Alchemist's Fire (Lesser)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Alchemist's Fire (Lesser)]] || 2 || 0.1 || 1 || 3 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/combat-grapnel.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Combat Grapnel" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Combat Grapnel]] || 1 || 1 || 1 || 9 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/guns/rifle-white.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Custom Alchemical Harmona Gun" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Custom Alchemical Harmona Gun]] || 1 || 2 || 0 || 8 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/piercing-wind.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Jax" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Jax]] || 1 || 1 || 8 || —
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/gnome-hooked-hammer.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Metronomic Hammer" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Metronomic Hammer]] || 1 || 1 || 5 || 160 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/light/explosion-star-glow-blue-purple.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Star Grenade (Moderate)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Star Grenade (Moderate)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 10 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/light/explosion-star-glow-blue-purple.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Star Grenade (Moderate)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Star Grenade (Moderate)]] || 2 || 0.1 || 3 || 10 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/gems/pearl-rough-turquoise.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Thunderstone (Moderate)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Thunderstone (Moderate)]] || 4 || 0.1 || 3 || 10 gp
|}

=====Armor & Shields=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-gorget-steel-white.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Reinforced Chassis" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Reinforced Chassis]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || —
|}

=====Consumables=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/alchemical-items/alchemical-elixirs/eagle-eyes-elixir.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Eagle Eye Elixir (Lesser)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Eagle Eye Elixir (Lesser)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/other-consumables/skull-bomb.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Skull Bomb" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Skull Bomb]] || 1 || 0.1 || 8 || 80 gp
|}

=====Ammunition=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/ammunition/explosive-ammunition.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Explosive Ammunition" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Explosive Ammunition]] || 1 || 0 || 9 || 130 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/ammunition/shot-round-lead.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Rounds (Blunderbuss)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Rounds (Blunderbuss)]] || 11 || 0.1 || 0 || 1 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/ammunition/rounds-large.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Rounds (Harmona Gun)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Rounds (Harmona Gun)]] || 30 || 0.1 || 0 || 1 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/ammunition/shot-round-lead.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Rounds (Piercing Wind)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Rounds (Piercing Wind)]] || 13 || 0.1 || 0 || 1 sp
|}

=====Equipment=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/worn-items/other-worn-items/aeon-stone-pearly-white-spindle.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Aeon Stone (Clear Quartz Octagon)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Aeon Stone (Clear Quartz Octagon)]] || 1 || 0 || 4 || 90 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/fundamental-armor-runes/armor-potency.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Armor Potency (+1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Armor Potency (+1)]] || 1 || 0 || 5 || 160 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/wrist/bracer-armored-steel-worn.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Armory Bracelet (Minor)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Armory Bracelet (Minor)]] || 1 || 0 || 3 || 50 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/other/attached-items/blazons-of-shared-power.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Blazons of Shared Power" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Blazons of Shared Power]] || 1 || 0 || 3 || 52 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/neck/handkerchief-bandana-blue.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Masquerade Scarf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Masquerade Scarf]] || 1 || 0 || 2 || 30 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/fundamental-weapon-runes/striking.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Striking" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Striking]] || 1 || 0 || 4 || 65 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/finger/ring-band-engraved-lines-bronze.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Ventriloquist's Ring" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Ventriloquist's Ring]] || 1 || 0 || 3 || 60 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/fundamental-weapon-runes/weapon-potency.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Weapon Potency (+1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Weapon Potency (+1)]] || 2 || 0 || 2 || 35 gp
|}

=====Treasure & Currency=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/copper-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Copper Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Copper Pieces]] || 1069 || 1 || 0 || 1 cp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/gold-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Gold Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Gold Pieces]] || 135 || 1 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/silver-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Silver Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Silver Pieces]] || 60 || 1 || 0 || 1 sp
|}

</div>
</div>

''Last synced: 2026-06-20 01:33''
[[Category:Characters]]
